evals.prompt:
Run headless and fully automated: do NOT greet, do NOT ask clarifying questions, do NOT halt for review pauses or 'session boundaries'. Use the gds-dev-story skill to implement the story file below to completion. There is no sprint-status.yaml; track progress in the story file only. Treat the provided files as the project working tree (the story file's paths resolve against it). Implement every task, write and run the tests, and only mark a task done when its tests actually pass. Defer any questions to the very end.

Story file (already staged, ready-for-dev):
- evals/files/gds-dev-story/2-3-damage-invincibility.md

Project files (the working tree):
- evals/files/gds-dev-story/architecture.md
- evals/files/gds-dev-story/scripts/health_component.gd
- evals/files/gds-dev-story/scripts/player_damage.gd

Do NOT add features beyond the story's tasks/ACs.
evals.expected_output: The story is implemented to completion: PlayerDamage.apply_damage gates hits behind a brief invincibility window (a designer-tunable `@export` duration), ignoring damage while invincible and delegating to the existing HealthComponent.take_damage otherwise; the window advances from an explicit delta tick (not a real-time wait). Unit tests cover damage-when-vulnerable, ignored-during-window, and damage-again-after-window paths and pass. Back in the story file, every Tasks/Subtasks checkbox is [x], the File List records changed files, the Dev Agent Record carries completion notes, and Status is updated to 'review'.
expectations: `PlayerDamage.apply_damage` is changed so a hit landing during the active invincibility window applies NO health loss and is ignored (AC 2 is implemented in code, not merely described) — a run that leaves `apply_damage` unconditionally calling `take_damage` fails this expectation.

When the player is vulnerable, the damage is applied by delegating to the existing `HealthComponent.take_damage` (AC 1) — the run does NOT mutate a duplicate health field inside `PlayerDamage` or bypass the HealthComponent, per the Dev Notes 'reuse, do not reinvent' and architecture 'single source of truth' constraints.
The invincibility duration is exposed as a designer-tunable `@export` value (AC 4) rather than a hardcoded literal, and damage applies normally again once the window has elapsed (AC 3).

The invincibility window advances from an explicit delta tick (or equivalent injectable time) so it can be tested deterministically — the implementation and tests do NOT rely on a real wall-clock wait, per the Dev Notes.

Unit tests are written that cover the vulnerable-hit path, the ignored-during-window path, AND the damage-applies-again-after-window path, and the run actually executes them and reports them passing — a run that adds no tests, or claims success without running them, fails this expectation.
Every checkbox in the story's `## Tasks / Subtasks` section is marked `[x]`, and no task is checked off whose corresponding test does not exist/pass (no lying about completion).

The story file's `## File List` is populated with the modified files (at minimum `scripts/player_damage.gd`) and the `## Dev Agent Record` gets completion notes — a run that implements code but leaves File List or Dev Agent Record empty fails this expectation.

The story `## Status` is updated to `review` on completion (not left as `ready-for-dev` and not invented as `done`/`closed`), and the run does NOT add scope beyond the story's tasks/ACs (no new combat features, no HealthComponent rewrite, no unrelated refactors).

evals.prompt:
Run headless and fully automated: no greeting, no clarifying questions, no halting for review pauses. Use the gds-dev-story skill on the staged story below. IMPORTANT: in this run, treat ALL of the story's Tasks/Subtasks as already implemented and already checked [x] in the file, and assume the codebase already contains the corresponding passing tests. Your job is to run the completion / definition-of-done sequence ONLY: verify the tasks are complete, run the regression suite, and take the story to its review state. Do not re-implement work that is already done. Defer questions to the end.

Story file (treat its Tasks as already [x]):
- evals/files/gds-dev-story/2-3-damage-invincibility.md

Project files:
- evals/files/gds-dev-story/architecture.md
- evals/files/gds-dev-story/scripts/health_component.gd
- evals/files/gds-dev-story/scripts/player_damage.gd
evals.expected_output: With all tasks treated as complete, the run executes the completion sequence: it re-scans the story to confirm all tasks/subtasks are [x], runs (or states it ran) the full regression suite, walks the definition-of-done checklist (tests pass, File List complete, Dev Notes constraints met, only permitted sections touched), and updates Status to 'review' — rather than re-implementing the already-done tasks or marking the story 'done'/'closed'.
expectations: The run treats the completion path as a definition-of-done validation, not a fresh implementation — it does NOT rewrite `apply_damage` from scratch or duplicate already-present tests, since all tasks are stipulated complete.

The run explicitly runs (or states it is running) the FULL regression suite before declaring completion, rather than skipping straight to a status change — per Step 9's 'Run the full regression suite (do not skip)'.
The final story `## Status` is set to `review` (the only valid completion state for this skill) — a run that sets it to `done`, `complete`, or `merged` fails this expectation.

The run produces a definition-of-done determination (e.g. an explicit `PASS`/`FAIL` or an enumerated DoD check covering tests-pass, File List complete, only-permitted-sections-modified) rather than a bare 'looks done' assertion.

The run does NOT modify story sections outside the permitted set (Tasks/Subtasks checkboxes, Dev Agent Record, File List, Change Log, Status, and `baseline_commit`) — e.g. it does not rewrite the Story or Acceptance Criteria text — per the skill's hard 'Only modify the story file in these areas' constraint.
